Kurdran Clawfinger is a level thirty-seven Dwarven Scholar
    [Retired].
Last login 23 years, 2 months, and 5 days ago from *.dk.
The thing is, you cannot judge a race. Any man who judges by the group is a
peawit. There is many a man alive no more to value than a dead dog, believe me,
but no two things alive are equal or have an equal chance; not a leaf, not a
tree. Where have you ever noted this magnificent equality? I do not go to war
to become anyone's equal, nor to make them mine. What I am fighting for is
the right to prove I am a better dwarf than most of them, but I do not think
race, king, lineage nor heritage matters a damn. What matters is justice, which
is why I am here. I will be treated as I deserve, not as my father deserved.

I am Kurdran, and I damn all gentlemen!

Orodreth of Tol Sírion is an Elf [Retired].
Last login 13 years, 11 months, and 17 days ago.

To the Sea, to the Sea!
The white gulls are wailing, the wind is roaring, and the white foam is flying.
Westward, westward the sun's round is falling.

Grey ship, grey ship, hearst-thou them calling;
the voices of my people who have gone whither I am going?

I will depart, I will depart from the forests of my birth;
for our days are ending, and our years are fading away.

I will fare across the wide waters, lonely sailing.
Long are the waves falling on the Last Shore,
sweet are the voices calling from the Lost Isle,
from Eressëa, from Elvenhome for mortals unfindable,
where be leaves unfalling: Land of my people for ever!
